title: State-space degradation residuals instead of fixed tyre-life windows
summary: Recent F1 tire research models latent degradation as a state that evolves through the stint with resets at pit stops.
rationale: Recent F1 tire research models latent degradation as a state that evolves through the stint with resets at pit stops. That suggests using per-row residualized degradation state features rather than static expected-life constants by compound.
implementation_hint: Estimate leakage-safe latent state proxies within each driver-race-stint using only past laps: cumulative positive lap-time drift, rolling residual from race-lap median pace, degradation acceleration, and post-pit reset-normalized state. Feed these to a specialist or append as compact features.
expected_effect: Improves discrimination when identical TyreLife values mean different true wear states because of compound, track, weather, or traffic.
risk: Sequence features can overfit noisy lap-time outliers and become validation-leaky if race-lap medians use future laps or same-fold full-race information.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
